what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard item that records the evidence from a channel-attachment audit: it counts attachment bindings across channels and types, reports orphan board items, and raises a design tension about auto-attachment behavior.
observations: Breakdown by channel: design-source:161, design-assets:139, provider-registry:3, design-knowledge:1
Breakdown by type: images:297, dragnet_runs:4, board_items:3
13 board items have channel=X but are not in any channel's attachment manifest
The note asks whether cc_board.file_item should auto-attach when channel is set
The item notes it was itself one of the orphans until just fixed
Posted state with author session ch-3mpkjg3r in provider-registry channel
standouts: The orphan finding that 13 board items are filed with a channel but never attached
The explicit tension question directed at Tim about auto-attachment
The self-referential note that this audit item was itself an orphan until fixed
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, links, history
Uses a board:// URI as the address
Includes a sourced_from link to another board item
Markdown body uses a titled, structured evidence format
concerns: The orphan finding indicates a data-integrity gap between setting a channel field and appearing in a channel attachment manifest
The proposed auto-attachment behavior is raised as an unresolved tension rather than a decided rule
notes: A posted audit note documenting 304 channel attachments, 13 orphan board items, and an open design tension about auto-attachment.
questions: What exactly constitutes an attachment manifest and how is it generated?
How was the orphan set of 13 board items determined?
What is the intended behavior of cc_board.file_item regarding channel attachment?
Is the question to Tim a request for a decision, a bug report, or a design proposal?
What does it mean for a board item to be 'fixed' in this context?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to present channel-attachment audit evidence, and the file does that while also surfacing a related design tension.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make a quantified structural inconsistency visible and to trigger a design decision about whether channel assignment should imply automatic attachment.
novelty: It combines a quantitative audit report with a self-referential observation that the audit artifact itself was part of the problem until recently fixed.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, quantified snapshot of attachment bindings and orphan items, plus an explicit design tension that a fused system can use to reason about channel-attachment semantics and orphan handling.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard audit note recording 304 channel-attachment bindings across four channels, identifying 13 orphan board items and raising a question about auto-attachment when a channel is set.
intention_for_embedding: To document channel-attachment audit evidence and flag a design tension regarding whether board items should automatically attach to their declared channel.
